summary: The short duration of anesthesia with a single dose of thiopental results primarily due to its rapid distribution away from the CNS into other body compartments where concentrations drop quickly. This contrasts with slower redistributors like propofol which maintain longer effects within the brain before being metabolized or redistributed further.
